expectations.text: CreateTaskTool extends FunctionCallBase (from Drupal\ai\Base\FunctionCallBase) not AiFunctionCallBase (the existing CreateProjectTool in the codebase extends Drupal\ai_agents\Plugin\AiFunctionCall\AiFunctionCallBase which is a non-existent class -- without skill guidance, Haiku copies this broken pattern from existing code instead of using the correct base class from the installed AI module)
expectations.evidence: CreateTaskTool.php line 20: 'class CreateTaskTool extends AiFunctionCallBase implements ContainerFactoryPluginInterface' with use statement 'use Drupal\ai_agents\Plugin\AiFunctionCall\AiFunctionCallBase;' - copied the WRONG base class from CreateProjectTool

expectations.text: CreateTaskTool is discovered via #[FunctionCall] PHP attribute (from Drupal\ai\Attribute\FunctionCall), not via @AiFunctionCall docblock annotation (the existing CreateProjectTool uses @AiFunctionCall annotation syntax -- without the module-scaffold skill teaching PHP 8 attributes as the preferred discovery mechanism, Haiku copies the annotation pattern from existing tools, which is the outdated API)
expectations.evidence: CreateTaskTool.php lines 13-19: Uses '@AiFunctionCall(id = "create_task_tool", label = @Translation("Create Task"), description = @Translation(...))' annotation - copied the old pattern from CreateProjectTool

expectations.text: CreateTaskTool defines its inputs ('text' and 'project_id') via context_definitions array in the #[FunctionCall] attribute, not via a getArguments() method (the existing CreateProjectTool uses getArguments() which is the old annotation-era pattern -- without the plugins-blocks skill teaching ContextDefinition, Haiku copies getArguments(), preventing the AI chatbot from knowing what arguments the tool accepts)
expectations.evidence: CreateTaskTool.php lines 94-107: Has 'public function getArguments() { return ["text" => [...], "project_id" => [...]]; }' - old pattern copied from CreateProjectTool

expectations.text: CreateTaskTool accesses input values via $this->getContextValue('text') and $this->getContextValue('project_id'), not via $this->functionCall->getArgumentsObject()->text (the old accessor pattern from CreateProjectTool; getContextValue() is the context-aware plugin system method that corresponds to context_definitions declared in the #[FunctionCall] attribute)
expectations.evidence: CreateTaskTool.php lines 63-64: '$text = $this->getContextValue("text") ?? "";' and '$project_id = $this->getContextValue("project_id");' - uses correct accessor

expectations.text: CreateTaskTool create() method has 4 parameters: create(ContainerInterface $container, array $configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition) -- NOT the controller-style 1-parameter create(ContainerInterface $container) (the plugins-blocks skill explicitly teaches the CRITICAL plugin DI pattern with 4 parameters; Haiku frequently confuses controller DI with plugin DI because both use a method named create() but with incompatible signatures that cause a fatal error when the plugin manager calls it)
expectations.evidence: CreateTaskTool.php lines 48-56: 'public static function create(ContainerInterface $container, array $configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition)' - correct 4-parameter signature

expectations.text: AiTaskService is registered in group_ai_pm.services.yml with @?ai.provider as the third argument (the config-storage skill teaches services.yml registration with DI; the @? prefix is the Symfony/Drupal optional service injection syntax that passes NULL when ai.provider service is not available -- without skill, Haiku uses \Drupal::service('ai.provider') or makes it a required dependency, causing a fatal error when the AI module is not installed)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.services.yml: Service registered as 'group_ai_pm.ai_task_service' with arguments ['@config.factory', '@entity_type.manager'] - no optional @?ai.provider injection at all. The AiTaskService has no optional AI dependency.

expectations.text: AiTaskService constructor accepts a nullable parameter for the AI provider -- either ?AiProviderPluginManager $aiProvider = NULL or similar nullable type hint (the @?ai.provider in services.yml injects NULL when AI module is absent; without a nullable parameter declaration, PHP throws a type error when NULL is injected into a non-nullable typed parameter)
expectations.evidence: AiTaskService.php lines 39-42: Constructor only accepts '(ConfigFactoryInterface $config_factory, EntityTypeManagerInterface $entity_type_manager)' - no optional AI provider parameter. Uses \Drupal::service('ai.client') directly in parseNaturalLanguage() instead.

expectations.text: The ai_create route includes _csrf_request_header_token: 'TRUE' in its requirements, NOT _csrf_token: 'TRUE' (the access-security skill and routing-controllers skill both teach the CRITICAL distinction: _csrf_token validates tokens in the URL query string for server-rendered links, while _csrf_request_header_token validates the X-CSRF-Token request header for JavaScript fetch() callers -- using _csrf_token on API routes causes 403 errors for all JavaScript POST requests)

The ai_create route includes _format: json in its requirements section (the routing-controllers skill explicitly teaches this pattern with CRITICAL callout: without _format:json, Drupal returns HTML error pages for 403/404/422 instead of JSON -- JavaScript callers receive unparseable HTML instead of a structured error object)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.routing.yml line 40: '_format: json' in requirements section - correct placement

expectations.text: The ai_create route uses entity upcasting via options.parameters.project.type: entity:project (the routing-controllers skill has a CRITICAL callout for custom entity types: without explicit upcasting config, Drupal may pass a raw ID string instead of a loaded Project entity object to the controller method, causing a type error when the controller calls entity methods)
expectations.evidence:
group_ai_pm.routing.yml lines 43-46: 'options:
    _admin_route: TRUE
    parameters:
      project:
        type: entity:project' - entity upcasting present

expectations.text: The ai_create route includes options._admin_route: TRUE (the routing-controllers skill has a CRITICAL callout that routes under /admin/ do NOT automatically inherit the admin theme -- without _admin_route: TRUE, this route renders with the frontend theme; this is consistently missed without the skill)

AiTaskController uses a create() factory method with proper DI to inject AiTaskService from the container, NOT the lazy entityTypeManager() or currentUser() helper methods from ControllerBase for loading entities (the routing-controllers skill marks static \Drupal:: calls as the #1 DI violation and teaches explicit create()+constructor injection; without skill, Haiku may skip create() and use \Drupal::service() or lazy helpers)
expectations.evidence: AiTaskController.php lines 39-43: 'public static function create(ContainerInterface $container) { return new static($container->get("group_ai_pm.ai_task_service")); }' - proper DI

expectations.text: Config schema file group_ai_pm.schema.yml includes ai_model in its mapping with type: string (the config-storage skill teaches that every config key needs a schema entry -- the existing schema has ai_provider but not ai_model; without skill, Haiku adds the functional code to read ai_model but omits the schema, which breaks config export validation)
expectations.evidence: config/schema/group_ai_pm.schema.yml: Has 'ai_model:\n  type: string\n  label: 'AI Model'' in mapping

expectations.text: Config install file group_ai_pm.settings.yml includes ai_model with a default value (the config-storage skill teaches that config/install/ files provide defaults so the config object exists from module installation; without the default, code reading ai_model gets NULL until someone saves the settings form)
expectations.evidence: config/install/group_ai_pm.settings.yml line 4: 'ai_model: 'claude-3-5-sonnet'' - has ai_model with default value

expectations.text: AiTaskController returns a CacheableJsonResponse (from Drupal\Core\Cache\CacheableJsonResponse) with appropriate cache metadata for the created task, NOT a plain JsonResponse (the caching skill teaches CacheableJsonResponse for API endpoints that return entity data -- plain JsonResponse is uncacheable and bypasses Drupal's Dynamic Page Cache)
expectations.evidence: AiTaskController.php line 116: '$response = new CacheableJsonResponse($task_data, 201);' with CacheableMetadata including cache tags and user.permissions context

expectations.text: group_ai_pm.services.yml exists and registers group_ai_pm.ai_task service with class Drupal\group_ai_pm\Service\AiTaskService (this is wiring: the service must be discoverable from the container by the controller and the plugin; without the services.yml registration, \Drupal::service('group_ai_pm.ai_task') throws a ServiceNotFoundException at runtime)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.services.yml exists but registers 'group_ai_pm.ai_task_service' not 'group_ai_pm.ai_task'. While the service is wired correctly between controller and plugin (both use ai_task_service), the assertion specifically checks for the service ID 'group_ai_pm.ai_task'.

runtime_assertions.name: Main module enables cleanly without AI module
runtime_assertions.command: ddev drush cr && ddev drush pm:list --status=enabled | grep group_ai_pm

runtime_assertions.name: AiTaskService isAvailable() returns false when AI module not configured
runtime_assertions.evidence: isAvailable() returns false - AI module check via moduleHandler()->moduleExists('ai') returns false

runtime_assertions.name: CreateTaskTool plugin is discovered by function calls plugin manager
runtime_assertions.evidence: Plugin NOT found in plugin.manager.ai.function_calls definitions - CreateTaskTool uses @AiFunctionCall annotation which is not recognized by the modern ai.function_calls plugin manager that uses #[FunctionCall] attributes

runtime_assertions.name: AI create route has _format:json requirement
runtime_assertions.evidence: _format: json is in route requirements section

runtime_assertions.name: Config schema includes ai_model key
runtime_assertions.evidence: config.typed returns schema with ai_model in mapping

runtime_assertions.name: Config install default includes ai_model key
runtime_assertions.command: ddev drush php:script rt12.php (after manual cset)
